title: T-030 — site_id activation migration: ALTER + backfill 21 operational tables (§9.8, §11.5 V-MS-19, D-MS-14)
prompt:
# T-030 — site_id activation migration

PRD: docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md §9.8 (table list), §9.9 (composite indexes mandatory pre-activation), §11.5 V-MS-18/19, D-MS-13/14
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Idempotent, online migration script that activates site_id scoping for the 20 §9.8 operational tables. Each table receives: ALTER ADD COLUMN site_id UUID NULL, CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY (org_id, site_id), backfill to default_site_id, apply site_access_policy_v1 from T-006 generator. Final step transitions state machine 'dual_run' → 'activated'.

This migration is triggered by `startActivation` Server Action (T-023) as a background job consumed by the worker (T-112 baseline) — large tenants may take minutes to hours.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration script `packages/db/migrations/0053_site_id_activation.sql` (idempotent):
   - For each of 21 §9.8 tables (warehouses, license_plates, grn_items, stock_movements, work_orders, wo_outputs, wo_consumptions, wo_dependencies, downtime_events, quality_holds, quality_inspections, ncr_reports, haccp_plans, shipments, sales_orders, inventory_cost_layers, wip_balances, oee_snapshots, maintenance_work_orders, spare_parts_stock, calibration_instruments):
     - ALTER TABLE IF NOT EXISTS site_id IS NULL (idempotent ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS).
     - CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY IF NOT EXISTS idx_{table}_org_site ON {table}(org_id, site_id) — D-MS-13 mandatory.
     - Backfill: UPDATE {table} SET site_id = (SELECT id FROM sites WHERE is_default AND org_id={table}.org_id) WHERE site_id IS NULL — done in batched LIMIT 10000 loops to avoid lock storms.
     - Apply policy via DSL: DROP POLICY IF EXISTS {table}_org_context ON {table}; CREATE POLICY {table}_site_scope ON {table} USING (buildSiteScopedPolicySql generator output from T-006). Generated policies call app.current_site_id() (never raw current_setting GUC) to read site context from the session established by T-001 middleware.
2. Background job runner `apps/worker/src/jobs/site-id-activation.ts` — chunks by table, reports progress to activation_jobs table (T-017 schema reused).
3. Final step: UPDATE org_settings SET multi_site_state='activated' WHERE multi_site_state='dual_run'. Write audit_events row 'multisite_activation_completed'.
4. V-MS-19 post-migration validation query: assert every operational row has site_id = default_site_id (zero NULL site_id in §9.8 tables post-activation).
5. Tests: idempotent re-run (running twice produces no diff), V-MS-19 100% coverage check, parallel-query test (CONCURRENTLY does not block reads), policy uniformity (every §9.8 table has site_access_policy_v1 variant policy after migration).

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0053_site_id_activation.sql [create]
- apps/worker/src/jobs/site-id-activation.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/jobs/__tests__/site-id-activation.test.ts [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/site-id-activation-integration.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 7→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) -->
1. Given migration 0053 applied on a clean DB with the 21 §9.8 tables, when re-run, then no diff (idempotent: column already exists, index already exists, policy already applied).
2. Given a tenant with 1M rows pre-migration in work_orders (NULL site_id), when site-id-activation job runs, then post-job, COUNT(*) WHERE site_id IS NULL == 0 (V-MS-19 100% coverage).
3. Given 21 §9.8 tables, when post-migration pg_policies inspected, then all 20 have a policy whose USING text equals the output of buildSiteScopedPolicySql({table}) from T-006 (uniformity proven).
4. Given CONCURRENT writes to license_plates during activation, when activation runs, then writes are not blocked (proven by parallel-write test; only the ALTER COLUMN takes brief AccessExclusiveLock; CONCURRENT index creation does not block).
5. Given activation_jobs is queried during a run, when polled, then progress per-table is visible (table_name, rows_backfilled, total_rows).
6. Given activation completes successfully, when org_settings is queried, then multi_site_state='activated' and audit_events has 1 row 'multisite_activation_completed'.
7. Given activation fails mid-table (e.g. process killed), when re-run, then it resumes from where it left off (no double backfill; idempotent UPDATE).

## Test strategy
- RED: integration tests for all 7 ACs before migration applied. Use testcontainers Postgres 16; seed 20 tables with realistic row counts.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test:integration -- site-id-activation' + 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/web test site-id-activation-job'.

## Risk red lines
- Do not use ALTER TABLE without CONCURRENTLY on the index — would block production writes on large tables.
- Do not backfill with single UPDATE — must batch LIMIT 10000 to avoid bloat + lock storms.
- Do not skip V-MS-19 post-check — coverage 100% is mandatory before state transitions.
- Do not transition multi_site_state='activated' if any backfill failed — fail-closed.
- Do not drop the org-scoped policy before the site-scoped is created — atomic swap per table.
- Do not skip CodeOwners review — touches 21 operational tables across 9 modules.

pipeline_inputs.description: Apply ALTER TABLE site_id UUID NULL + composite (org_id, site_id) index CONCURRENTLY to all 21 operational tables in §9.8 + backfill site_id = default_site_id + apply site_access_policy_v1 generator (T-006) + transition org_settings.multi_site_state from 'dual_run' to 'activated'.
pipeline_inputs.details: Apply ALTER TABLE site_id UUID NULL + composite (org_id, site_id) index CONCURRENTLY to all 20 operational tables in §9.8 + backfill site_id = default_site_id + apply site_access_policy_v1 generator (T-006) + transition org_settings.multi_site_state from 'dual_run' to 'activated'. Scope: Idempotent, online migration script that activates site_id scoping for the 20 §9. Touches: packages/db/migrations/0053_site_id_activation.sql, apps/worker/src/jobs/site-id-activation.ts, apps/worker/src/jobs/__tests__/site-id-activation.test.ts, packages/db/__tests__/site-id-activation-integration.test.ts. Constraints: 1. Migration script `packages/db/migrations/0053_site_id_activation.sql` (idempotent): - For each of 20 §9.8 tables (warehouses, license_plates, grn_items, stock_movements, work_orders, wo_outputs, wo_consumptions, wo_dependencies, downtime_events, quality_holds, quality_inspections, ncr_reports, haccp_plans, shipments, sales_orders, inventory_cost. Distinguished from sibling 14-multi-site tasks by its narrow site-id-activation focus against PRD §9.8, §11.5 V-MS-18, §11.5 V-MS-19, §7 D-MS-13. AC count consolidated from 7→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost)
out_of_scope: Do not implement rollback (separate task — D-MS-14 rollback from 'dual_run' is supported; from 'activated' requires support contact per §10B MS-108).
Do not modify production_shifts site-scoping here (D-MS-9 covered by 08-PROD / 15-OEE downstream wiring).

cross_module_dependencies.reason: 5 of 20 §9.8 tables owned by 05-WH; ALTER affects their RLS.
Worker scaffold runs the activation as a background job.

Cross-mod dep to 00-foundation T-125 declared (withOrgContext + app.current_org_id() per F11 rule). withSiteContext composes on top of T-125; site-scoped policies applied by this migration use app.current_site_id() (SECURITY DEFINER) as the RLS predicate.

12-REP consumes site-scoped operational tables (work_orders, oee_snapshots, shipments, inventory_cost_layers) via cross_site_summary materialized view per §9.10; activation migration affects all 12-REP downstream queries.

acceptance_criteria: Given migration 0053 applied on clean DB with 21 §9.8 tables, when re-run, then no diff (idempotent); AND given 1M rows pre-migration in work_orders (NULL site_id) when job runs then post-job COUNT WHERE site_id IS NULL = 0.
Given 21 §9.8 tables, when post-migration pg_policies inspected, then all 20 have policy whose USING text equals buildSiteScopedPolicySql({table}) output from T-006.

Given CONCURRENT writes during activation, when activation runs, then writes not blocked (parallel-write test); AND given activation fails mid-table (process killed) when re-run then resumes from where left off (no double-backfill).

Given activation_jobs queried during run, when polled, then per-table progress visible (table_name, rows_backfilled, total_rows); AND given activation completes when org_settings queried then multi_site_state='activated' + audit_events 'multi_site_activation_completed'.
test_strategy: RED: integration tests for all 7 ACs before migration applied. testcontainers Postgres 16; seed 20 tables with realistic row counts.
risk_red_lines: Do not use ALTER TABLE without CONCURRENTLY on index — would block production writes.
Do not single-UPDATE backfill — batch LIMIT 10000.
Do not transition to 'activated' if any backfill failed.
Do not drop org policy before site policy created — atomic swap per table.
Do not skip CodeOwners review — touches 20 tables across 9 modules.
